families.name: Against the person
families.prompt: It seems to answer the person or a reworded version of their point, more than the point itself

families.name: Leans on something beside the evidence
families.prompt: It seems to lean on a feeling, a name, naturalness, or popularity more than on reasons

families.name: Leaps past the evidence
families.prompt: The conclusion seems to reach further than the evidence given carries it

families.name: Assumes what it should establish
families.prompt: It seems to take its starting point for granted instead of supporting it

families.name: Changes the subject
families.prompt: It seems to dodge the point rather than answer it

families.name: Plays on feelings
families.prompt: It seems to move you by emotion rather than by reasons

families.name: Leans on who or how many
families.prompt: It seems to rest on authority, popularity, or novelty rather than evidence

families.name: Forces a narrow choice
families.prompt: It frames the options more narrowly than reality allows

families.name: Assumes what it's proving
families.prompt: Its support seems to already take the conclusion for granted

families.name: Shifts the burden of proof
families.prompt: It treats 'not disproven' as 'proven', or vice versa

families.name: Jumps from too little
families.prompt: It draws a broad conclusion from thin or one-sided evidence

families.name: Mistakes the cause
families.prompt: It reads a cause into things that may just be linked or sequential

families.name: Compares unlike things
families.prompt: It leans on an analogy or comparison that may not hold

families.name: Slides between meanings
families.prompt: A key word seems to shift meaning, or the phrasing is doing the work

families.name: Confuses parts and wholes
families.prompt: It assumes the parts and the whole must share a trait

families.name: Conditional slips
families.prompt: It draws the wrong move from an if-then

families.name: Misreads the numbers
families.prompt: It draws a shaky conclusion from statistics or probability

families.name: Other ways to miss the point
families.prompt: It answers something adjacent to, but not, the actual claim

families.name: Other things taken for granted
families.prompt: It smuggles in an assumption the argument needed to earn

families.name: Other misplaced appeals
families.prompt: It rests on a source of pull that does not bear on truth

families.name: Other word games
families.prompt: Vague, loaded, or weasel wording does the work
cues: where do you draw the line

ad_hominem.text: Engages the claim itself, not the character or motives of who made it
Weighs the claim on its own merits, not on reasons to distrust the speaker
Actually addresses the original point rather than shifting focus to the speaker

strawman.text: Argues against the claim as actually made, not an exaggerated or absolute version
Restates the other side's position in a way they would recognize and accept

tu_quoque.text: Answers the criticism on its merits, not by pointing at the critic's own conduct
Any inconsistency it raises is genuinely the issue, not just a way to deflect

appeal_to_authority.text: Rests on reasons you could check, not mainly on who endorsed it
Any cited expert actually fits the topic and isn't treated as the whole case
The case would hold without the prestige of the name attached to it

appeal_to_emotion.text: A real reason remains once the emotional pull (fear, pity, anger) is set aside
Persuades by reasons that would still stand if the case were put calmly
The push toward the conclusion is a reason, not mainly a feeling

appeal_to_nature.text: Judges good or bad by actual effects, not by the 'natural' or 'artificial' label
Checks this specific case for benefit or harm, not naturalness alone
Ties its verdict to measured benefit or harm, not 'natural means good'

bandwagon.text: Rests on reasons, not on how many people believe or do it
Gives independent evidence beyond 'most people' or 'everyone' agreeing

hasty_generalization.text: The breadth of the conclusion matches how much evidence backs it
The examples look representative, not cherry-picked or one-off anecdotes

false_cause.text: Offers a mechanism or evidence for the cause, beyond just timing or correlation
Considers and rules out other explanations (coincidence, a shared cause)
The case is more than 'one came first, so it caused the other'

slippery_slope.text: The steps from the first move to the feared outcome are actually supported
The predicted outcome is proportionate to the small step being discussed
Each link in the chain is shown to be likely, not just assumed to follow

false_dilemma.text: Presents the real range of options, not just two when a 'both' or 'neither' exists
Leaves a workable middle ground or 'do both' on the table
Allows positions beyond exactly two camps

circular_reasoning.text: The support is an independent reason, not a restatement of the conclusion
Would persuade someone who doesn't already accept the conclusion
The reason adds something the conclusion did not already say

argument_from_incredulity.text: Rejects the claim on evidence, not because the speaker can't picture how it could be true
Gives an actual reason against the claim, not just 'I don't see how'
The doubt points to something implausible about the claim, not a gap in imagination

poisoning_the_well.text: Takes up the point after it's made, rather than labeling the source beforehand
Leaves each thing the person says open to be judged on its own merits

genetic_fallacy.text: Judges the claim by what it says now, not by where or whom it came from
Examines the idea's present merit regardless of how or where it started

red_herring.text: Stays on the topic actually under discussion
The point it raises bears on the original question

whataboutism.text: Responds to the criticism on its own terms

moving_the_goalposts.text: Accepts the originally requested evidence as settling the point
Holds to the standard it stated up front

non_sequitur.text: The conclusion follows from the reasons offered
Spells out the step connecting reasons to conclusion

appeal_to_fear.text: Backs the warning with evidence the outcome is likely, not just how scary it is
Sizes the danger to what's actually known, not inflated for effect

appeal_to_pity.text: Gives reasons the conclusion holds, not just sympathy for a hardship
Any hardship it raises genuinely bears on what's being decided

appeal_to_ridicule.text: Gives a reason the position is wrong, not just a laugh at its expense
Engages the position as meant rather than a mocking caricature of it

appeal_to_spite.text: Rests on reasons the conclusion is right, not on someone getting their comeuppance
Any wrongdoing it raises genuinely bears on what's being decided

appeal_to_tradition.text: Rests on reasons it works, not on how long or how traditionally it's been done
Shows what the long practice has actually produced, beyond merely lasting

appeal_to_novelty.text: Rests on reasons it performs better, not on how new or modern it is
Shows a real improvement over the older option, beyond merely being latest

snob_appeal.text: Rests on the thing's actual qualities, not the elite status of who picks it
Offers reasons the thing is genuinely good, not just a place among a select set

false_equivalence.text: Compares things that truly match in scale and seriousness
Limits the comparison to the respect the two things actually share

perfect_solution.text: Weighs the leftover flaws against the real gains the option offers
Judges the option against realistic alternatives, not an unreachable ideal

middle_ground.text: Backs the moderate position with its own reasons, not just its central location
Lets the better-supported side win, even when that side is an extreme

begging_the_question.text: Supports the premises it leans on rather than taking the crucial one for granted
Argues the contested point out in the open instead of assuming it settled

loaded_question.text: Leaves the contested point genuinely open rather than baking it into the question
Can be answered straight without first conceding an unproven assumption

appeal_to_ignorance.text: Gives positive evidence for the claim, not just the lack of evidence against it
Treats an unproven claim as open, not as settled false for want of proof

shifting_burden_of_proof.text: The one making the claim provides the support, rather than asking for it to be disproven
Lets the claim earn acceptance by being backed up, not stand by default until refuted

argument_from_silence.text: Builds on what the source actually says, not on a gap where it doesn't mention something
Shows the source would truly be expected to mention it before reading meaning into its silence

anecdotal.text: Backs the claim with systematic evidence, not just a personal story or one experience
Lets the broader data carry the conclusion rather than one vivid case

cherry_picking.text: Acknowledges and weighs evidence that points the other way, not just the helpful bits
Shows the full mix of cases, not only the ones that fit the conclusion

sampling_bias.text: Draws its sample to represent the whole, not just whoever was handy or volunteered
Samples cases that match the population the conclusion is about

overgeneralization.text: Qualifies the rule to where it actually holds instead of claiming it never fails
Allows for exceptions rather than forcing the rule onto a case that doesn't fit

post_hoc.text: Offers evidence beyond the sheer order of events, not just 'it came after'
Shows the sequence repeats reliably, not a single coincidental before-and-after

single_cause.text: Treats the outcome as having several contributing factors, not one sole cause
Acknowledges and weighs other contributing causes rather than ignoring them

correlation_causation.text: Gives evidence that one thing produces the other, beyond their tracking together
Establishes which way the causation runs, not just that the two move in step

weak_analogy.text: Points to shared traits that genuinely bear on the conclusion, not just surface resemblance
The similarities run deep enough to actually carry the conclusion drawn

false_analogy.text: The two cases match in the respect the conclusion depends on, with no decisive difference skipped
The parallel holds right at the point the argument turns on, not just elsewhere

apples_to_oranges.text: Measures both against one shared standard that genuinely fits each of them
The two are alike enough in kind that lining them up side by side actually means something

equivocation.text: Keeps each key word in one consistent sense from premise to conclusion
Still works when the key term is pinned to a single fixed definition throughout

amphiboly.text: Relies on a sentence that has one clear grammatical reading, not two
Phrases the load-bearing sentence so only one meaning survives

accent.text: Reads the statement under its plain emphasis, not a stress that tilts the meaning
Means the same with the original context and emphasis restored

definist.text: Defines the key term independently, not in a way that pre-decides the conclusion
Uses a definition that matches the term's ordinary or agreed meaning

composition.text: Establishes the whole's trait directly, not by copying it up from each part
Shows why the property survives combining the parts, rather than assuming it carries up

division.text: Establishes each part's trait directly, not by handing it down from the whole
Shows why the property reaches each member, rather than assuming it distributes down

ecological_fallacy.text: Keeps a group average as a group claim, not a verdict about one individual
Backs claims about the person with individual-level evidence, not just the group rate

affirming_the_consequent.text: Allows the predicted result could arrive without the stated condition being true
Checks and rules out other routes to the same result before affirming the condition

denying_the_antecedent.text: Allows the result could still arrive another way even with the stated condition absent
Shows the stated condition really is the only path before ruling the result out

base_rate_neglect.text: Weighs how common the thing is, not just the test result or sign on its own
Folds the rarity of the condition into the odds rather than reading a positive as near-certain

gamblers_fallacy.text: Keeps each independent trial at the same odds, not swayed by a recent streak
Doesn't treat the opposite outcome as 'due' just because a run went one way

regression_fallacy.text: Notices when a case was singled out at an extreme, where a swing back toward normal is expected
Rules out plain drift back to average before crediting the action for the change

survivorship_bias.text: Counts the cases that failed or dropped out, not only the survivors still in view
Checks whether the missing failures shared the same trait before crediting it for success

texas_sharpshooter.text: Names the pattern in advance instead of drawing the target around a cluster after seeing it
Weighs all the cases together rather than spotlighting one cluster and ignoring the misses

appeal_to_consequences.text: Ties the verdict to evidence the claim is true, not to how pleasant believing it would be
Rejects a claim only when something shows it false, not because its implication is unwelcome

appeal_to_motive.text: Weighs the claim on the evidence, not on what the speaker stands to gain
Treats a hidden motive as a cue to check the point, not as a reason it must be false

two_wrongs.text: Defends the act on its own merits, not by pointing to a similar wrong elsewhere
Judges the act on its own, treating 'others did it' as no excuse for it

ad_hominem_circumstantial.text: Addresses the view itself, not whether the speaker's situation makes it expected
Treats the speaker's situation as a bias to check, not as proof the view is wrong

complex_question.text: Asks about one matter at a time, not several fused into a single demand
Lets each part be answered separately rather than forcing one answer to settle both

no_true_scotsman.text: Lets a counterexample count against the claim rather than relabeling it away
Holds to membership terms set out beforehand, not ones added once the example appeared

suppressed_correlative.text: Keeps both sides of the pair meaningful rather than stretching one to swallow the other
Preserves a real line between the options instead of emptying one category out

appeal_to_force.text: Rests on reasons the claim is true, not on a cost threatened for disagreeing
Leaves you free to disagree on the merits, without penalty attached for doing so

appeal_to_flattery.text: Persuades with reasons a stranger could weigh, not by complimenting the listener
Still stands once any praise is set aside, because real reasons support it

appeal_to_wealth.text: Shows quality by how the thing performs, not by how much it costs
Weighs the claim on its reasons, not on how rich the person making it is

appeal_to_common_sense.text: Offers actual reasons for the claim rather than declaring it obvious or common sense
Answers doubts on their merits instead of casting the doubter as lacking sense

loaded_language.text: Backs the judgment with stated reasons, not just with words that pre-decide it
Still holds up when the charged terms are replaced with plain neutral ones

weasel_words.text: Names a specific source one could look up, not a vague 'some say'
Puts real evidence behind the claim rather than a softening qualifier

continuum_fallacy.text: Grants the real gap between clear cases even without a precise cutoff
Accepts that many small steps can add up to a genuine difference

reification.text: Uses 'society' or 'the market' as shorthand, not as a thing with a will of its own
Rests the conclusion on the concrete people and facts behind the abstraction

buckets.name: It misses the point
buckets.prompt: The response seems aimed somewhere other than the actual claim

buckets.name: It leans on the wrong thing
buckets.prompt: It seems to lean on feeling, popularity, authority, or tradition more than reasons

buckets.name: It assumes too much
buckets.prompt: It seems to take for granted something it should have established

buckets.name: It over-reaches the evidence
buckets.prompt: The conclusion seems to go further than the evidence supports

buckets.name: It plays on words
buckets.prompt: The wording itself seems to be doing something slippery

buckets.name: It mishandles the logic
buckets.prompt: The reasoning structure or the numbers themselves seem to go wrong
